because.text: Invited pedantry, earned pedantry. P1 confirmed: 'false | tail -1' exits 0 without pipefail, and the masked step was the ONE gate guarding Book III's two-implementation claim - a failed Go replay or differential would have shown green; fixed with the audit's exact proposal. Dual lesson to the 11-run red streak: audit CI for LYING GREEN, not just for red. P2s all accepted and shipped: Merkle residue in criterion 10 + reviews/README (the exact term the gate corrected, surviving where the correction missed); Book II s1 future-document promise -> s10/Book III; CHANGELOG s7-vs-s10 drift; README Two Books header/table/dup; Book I version-bundle ambiguity resolved by an explicit ANCHORS bundle convention (NOT by bumping Book I, which would fabricate a change); oracle-vs-bundle docstrings; suite README v1/39 -> v2/49 (oldest stale doc, predating three format changes); Warrant v0.1 wording made precise; proofs/README bridge-scope honesty (premise-check, not row correspondence; step-tag classifier recorded as future work); review-tooling briefs advanced to the full v0.6 surface - stale briefs would have blinded the next reviewer to exactly the new code. P3 typos fixed in the anchored Book III (part of the re-anchor). v0.6.1: zero behavior changes, every gate output byte-identical - which is the point.

subject.note: Codex pedantic full-state audit: 1 real CI P1 (tail-masking on the Book III gate step) + stale-reference sweep across 5 releases -> all shipped as v0.6.1
